Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results identify the candidate as Agr35256-1, later assigned as UGT94AX1, and the Accession numbers section gives GenBank accession LC704571 for AgApiT (UGT94AX1).

Enzyme_Name.reason: Discussion states this name has already been defined in EC 2.4.2.25 as 'UDP-Api:7-O-(β-D-glucosyl)-flavone apiosyltransferase'; this matches the provided enzyme name wording apart from UDP-apiose/UDP-Api notation.

EC_Number.reason: Discussion explicitly states the enzyme name has been defined already in EC 2.4.2.25.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state the gene contains a complete ORF of UGT with the C-terminal PSPG motif, was assigned to UGT94AX1, and candidate screening was based on glycoside-specific glycosyltransferases (GGTs).

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies celery and identifies UGT94AX1 from celery.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Materials and methods specify celery as A. graveolens; the abstract and text refer to Apium graveolens.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Results and Plant materials specify the cultivar 'New Cornell 619'.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in celery tissues and with the celery gene.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied organism in the paper is Apium graveolens.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The experimental celery cultivar used was 'New Cornell 619'.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The abstract and Results explicitly frame UGT94AX1/AgApiT as responsible for the last sugar modification step in apiin biosynthesis and producing apiose-containing flavone glycosides.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Abstract says it catalyzes the last sugar modification step in apiin biosynthesis; Discussion says it transfers apiose to flavone 7-O-glucosides.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The abstract explicitly identifies UGT94AX1 as catalyzing the last sugar modification step in apiin biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Recombinant UGT94AX1 converted apigenin 7-O-glucoside plus UDP-Api into apiin in vitro, directly forming the target product.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Discussion states AgApiT transfers the apiosyl residue from UDP-Api to the 2-position of the glucose residue of flavone 7-O-glucosides to produce apiosylated flavones including apiin; Results show activity on several flavone/flavonol 7-O-glucosides.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Results directly test UDP-Api as donor and apigenin 7-O-glucoside as acceptor, with kinetic parameters for both.

Primary_Product.reason: Results identify the reaction product from apigenin 7-O-glucoside + UDP-Api as authentic apiin by HPLC retention time.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The study centers on biosynthesis of apiin, and UGT94AX1 directly produces apiin in vitro.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Apiin is repeatedly described as a flavone glycoside/flavonoid glycoside, which falls under flavonoid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show apiin content is highest in true leaves and note apiin contents were also high in seeds.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Abstract and Results support that UGT94AX1 catalyzes the last sugar modification step of apiin biosynthesis and can produce various apiose-containing flavone glycosides in celery due to acceptor promiscuity.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper suggests apiin production may be regulated by AgApiT expression and that it is a terminal step, but it does not formally demonstrate or establish rate-limiting status.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported: candidate identification by coexpression/phylogeny, in vitro conversion to apiin, strict UDP-Api donor specificity with several acceptors, importance of Ile139/Phe140/Leu356, and inference that it is the sole celery ApiT gene.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state UGT94AX1 expression increased in early leaf development, peaked at stage 2, decreased thereafter, and was high in true leaves and low in other tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same expression pattern is shown for the experimental celery plants used in the study.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/Methods: RNA-seq screening, RT-qPCR, recombinant protein assay, HPLC comparison with authentic apiin, kinetic analysis, site-directed mutagenesis, and homology modeling.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Results analyze developmental stage effects, and Materials describe seedlings grown at 22°C under a 16-h light/8-h dark cycle.

Omics_Data.reason: Results and Methods explicitly cite SRR1023730 and DRR396538/396539/396540 RNA-seq datasets.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Discussion explicitly notes AgApiT has a relatively low kcat/low metabolic turnover and no organism-level growth tradeoff is reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results support that AgApiT is an inverting glycosyltransferase, has strict UDP-Api donor specificity with broader acceptor range, is inferred to be the only ApiT in celery, and that Ile139/Phe140/Leu356 are key for donor recognition and exclusion of UDP-Glc/UDP-Xyl.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The gene is referred to as AgFNSI, and the paper states apigenin—the aglycone of apiin—is synthesized by known apiin biosynthesis-related enzyme genes including AgFNSI; FNSI denotes flavone synthase I.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: This paper does not explicitly state that AgFNSI belongs to the 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase family, even if that is generally true for FNSI.

Source_Species.reason: AgFNSI is discussed as a celery gene.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The celery used is Apium graveolens.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Results specify the celery cultivar used in this study was 'New Cornell 619'.

Applied_Species.reason: Expression analysis was performed in celery tissues.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species is Apium graveolens.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results describe AgFNSI as a known apiin biosynthesis-related enzyme gene; Discussion also places FNSI among enzymes for apigenin biosynthesis relevant to apiin formation.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Discussion states FNSI encodes an enzyme for apigenin biosynthesis, which is upstream of apiin biosynthesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: AgFNSI is presented as an upstream apiin-biosynthesis-related enzyme compared with the terminal AgApiT step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: AgFNSI is linked indirectly to apiin by supplying the apigenin/flavone precursor pathway, as described in Results and Discussion.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results state apigenin, the aglycone of apiin, is synthesized at an early stage and Discussion says FNSI encodes an enzyme for apigenin biosynthesis; thus catalyzing upstream flavone formation leading to apigenin is directly inferable.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper explicitly says apigenin is the aglycone of apiin and discusses AgFNSI as an enzyme gene for apigenin biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AgFNSI is discussed in the context of apiin biosynthesis, making apiin the terminal metabolite linked to this upstream gene.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Apiin is described as a flavone/flavonoid glycoside, so flavonoid is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results show apiin is highest in true leaves and note high apiin in seeds as well.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper supports that AgFNSI is part of the apiin biosynthetic pathway and is coexpressed with UGT94AX1 during early leaf development, consistent with supporting apiin accumulation.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish this for AgFNSI, and the field value explicitly says 'not established in this paper,' which is faithful.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are consistent with the Results: AgFNSI expression parallels UGT94AX1, its pattern is consistent with a precursor-supplying role, and it is used as a known comparison gene rather than being newly biochemically characterized here.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state AgFNSI showed a similar expression pattern to UGT94AX1 in developmental stages and tissues, i.e., high in early true leaves and less in other tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This matches the RT-qPCR data shown for the experimental celery plants.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: For AgFNSI in this paper, the supporting method is RT-qPCR expression profiling compared with apiin accumulation and UGT94AX1 expression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: AgFNSI expression was analyzed across developmental stages of leaf growth.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that AgFNSI appears in Results as a known apiin-biosynthesis-related enzyme gene used for coexpression comparison and was not newly functionally characterized here.

corrections.reason: This paper does not explicitly state that AgFNSI belongs to the 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase family, even if that is generally true for FNSI.
